title: market-sizer output
description: Output of the f04 market-sizer agent (gpt-5.6-sol). size_bottom_up, size_top_down, growth and why_now are written verbatim as claims.value records (design.md 3.2). Every numeric field requires a matching evidence[] row carrying source_url and a word-for-word quote_verbatim; a number without one must not be emitted at all (design.md 9). The agent emits no score, band, verdict, implied exit value, outlook label or trend adjective — all of those are computed deterministically downstream (design.md 5, 6).

sizeRecord.description: Shape of design.md 3.2 market.size_bottom_up / market.size_top_down claim values, field for field.

tam_low.description: Conservative end of the TAM range, in `currency` units (absolute, not millions). CONSUMER: vc_rule_check TAM band (design.md 6.1 — applied to tam_low, NOT tam_high, because the generous end is what report-mill inflation attacks) and the venture-scale ceiling (design.md 6.2, tam_used = tam_low).

tam_high.description: Generous end of the TAM range. CONSUMER: memo Market sizing section (feature 06) and the dashboard TAM range display (feature 09). Never a point estimate paired with tam_low: both endpoints must derive from real sourced anchors.

currency.description: ISO 4217 of the source figures. Mixed-currency sources must be converted with the conversion stated in assumptions[], never silently mixed.

buyer_count.description: Count of buyer_unit in geography, from a document that states it. CONSUMER: the AUTHORITATIVE buyer_concentration derivation in the validator (design.md 6.2): < 10000 -> concentrated, 10000-500000 -> mid_market, > 500000 -> long_tail. This overrides the market-categorizer's non-authoritative hint; disagreement sets missing_flags.concentration_revised = true. Must NEVER be an incumbent-industry revenue figure (design.md 6.4).

buyer_count_source_url.description: Exact URL of the page carrying the buyer_count figure. Non-null whenever buyer_count is non-null — the validator drops numbers whose source does not resolve (design.md 9, REQ-004).

arpu_low.description: Low end of annual revenue per buyer, from real pricing evidence.

arpu_high.description: High end of annual revenue per buyer, from real pricing evidence.

arpu_basis.description: What the ARPU figure actually is, in plain language (e.g. 'published list price, Team tier, x12'). CONSUMER: memo Market sizing 'with stated assumptions' requirement (design.md 11 / feature 06).

assumptions.description: Load-bearing assumptions in plain language — which slice of the buyer population is addressable, whether the price applies to all of it, what was excluded and why, and any disagreement between sources that was reported as a spread rather than reconciled. CONSUMER: the memo prints this list verbatim (design.md 11); the brief requires market sizing to carry stated assumptions.

gaps.description: Free-text description of what was missing within this size model specifically. Distinct from the top-level data.gaps[], which is the structured, reason-coded channel the validator maps onto missing_flags.

code.description: Finite set; the agent may not invent codes. 'abstained' is a COMPLETED job (design.md 9: abstention is explicitly permitted and produces a missing claim rather than a guess).

ok.description: true for both 'success' and 'abstained'; false for 'insufficient_input' and 'processing_error'. CONSUMER: the n8n IF node routing to the gap-claim writer vs the error branch (design.md 4).

size_bottom_up.description: PRIMARY construction: buyer_count x ARPU. CONSUMER: market.size_bottom_up claim (design.md 3.2). null = abstention, which yields TAM band UNKNOWN (design.md 6.0/6.1), never FAIL.

size_top_down.description: CROSS-CHECK ONLY, never the primary (design.md 3.2). CONSUMER: market.size_top_down claim; may set the TAM band only with missing_flags.top_down_only = true and the confidence cap <= 0.45 (design.md 6.1, 6.5).

cagr_pct_low.description: Low end of the CAGR range, in percent (15.0 = 15%). CONSUMER: the CAGR band in vc_rule_check (design.md 6.1: PASS >= 15%, WATCH [5%,15%), FAIL < 5%, UNKNOWN when no growth figure was established).

cagr_pct_high.description: High end of the CAGR range, in percent.

basis.description: What the figure measures and where it came from, in plain language, including an explicit note when the only available CAGR is report-mill-shaped (clustering in the 15-25% band). CONSUMER: memo (feature 06).

why_now.description: CONSUMER: market.why_now claim (design.md 3.2). Claim-only — rendered in the memo (feature 06) and NOT an input to any design.md 6 formula. design.md 3.2 also specifies evidence_claim_ids[] on this claim value; that array is populated by the write node from the evidence rows this agent emits, not by the agent (it cannot know claim ids).

catalyst_kind.description: REQUIRED typed enum (design.md 3.2). Untyped -> the whole why_now object is null and a gap is declared instead.

catalyst_artifact_url.description: Concrete dated artifact — a specific regulation, model/hardware release, published price change. Uncited -> why_now is null and written as missing instead (design.md 3.2).

tailwinds.description: CONSUMER: one market.tailwind claim per item (design.md 3.2, value shape {statement}), item_key = sha256(statement) (design.md 3.5). Each item requires its own evidence[] row; unsourced items must not be emitted.

headwinds.description: CONSUMER: one market.headwind claim per item (design.md 3.2).

evidence.description: The provenance chain. CONSUMER: evidence rows (design.md 3.4/3.5). tier and strength are assigned by the validator from the deterministic domain map, never by this agent.

supports_field.description: Dotted path to a field actually populated in data, e.g. 'size_bottom_up.buyer_count', 'growth.cagr_pct_low', 'why_now', 'tailwinds[0]'. CONSUMER: the write node uses it to attach the evidence row to the correct claim.

source_url.description: Exact URL of the page carrying the figure. null ONLY on a relation='context' row recording that a search was performed and returned nothing (design.md 3.5: tier='missing' rows use relation='context' and carry NULL url and NULL quote).

quote_verbatim.description: The source's own sentence, WORD FOR WORD, unedited, untranslated, untidied. This is the RSK-003 verbatim layer against LLM re-centering of unusual claims (design.md 2, 3.2). null only on relation='context' rows.

published_date.description: From the document when present; null otherwise. Note: only topic='news' Tavily results carry published_date, so most rows here are null (design.md 3.5).

relation.description: Matches the evidence table's relation column. 'contradicts' is how a disagreeing source is recorded — a contradiction is data, not a flag, and it must never be smoothed into a mean (design.md 3.4 triangulate-don't-average, design.md 8). 'context' rows are EXCLUDED from the confidence evidence count (design.md 6.5).

gaps.description: MANDATORY explicit gap declaration — the deliberate inversion of company-research-agent's 'Never mention no information found' (design.md 9). CONSUMERS: *.gap claims with verification_status='missing' (design.md 3.2), scores.missing_flags (design.md 3.7), and missing_count in the confidence formula (design.md 6.5).

reason_code.description: Closed vocabulary so the validator maps gaps onto missing_flags deterministically. 'incumbent_anchored_tam_only' specifically sets missing_flags.incumbent_anchored_tam = true and forces the TAM band to UNKNOWN, never FAIL (design.md 6.4).

note.description: Should name the figure that was declined, so a human can audit the abstention rather than wonder why the field is empty. Rendered in the memo's honest-omission section (feature 06).

size_bottom_up.arpu_basis: Competitor published list price, annualised: Core $1,850/mo x 12; Enterprise $4,200/mo x 12
assumptions: Buyer count sources disagree: FDIC states 4,024 (Mar 2026), ICBA states 'roughly 4,400'. Reported as a spread, not reconciled to a mean.
ARPU anchored on a single competitor's list price; actual contract values are typically discounted and were not found in any document.
gaps: No source segments the addressable share of the buyer population.

evidence.quote_verbatim: As of March 31, 2026, there were 4,024 FDIC-insured community banks.

gaps.note: No non-weak document stated a market size for this category.
No dated artifact found behind the founder's 'AI makes this possible now' framing.

status.message: No admissible buyer-count anchor: the only quantitative anchor available was incumbent-industry revenue.

gaps.note: Only quantitative anchor found was 'Germany's courier, express and parcel industry generated EUR 27.1 billion in revenue in 2025' — incumbent-industry revenue, not a count of independent restaurants. Deriving TAM from it would size the industry being displaced (error of omission). Declined.
Only market-size figure came from an SEO listicle with no methodology and a 21.4% CAGR sitting in the 15-25% band typical of generated report content. Not emitted.
